title: lupinus pilosus var. velutinus
intro: Lupinus pilosus var. velutinus, also known as velvety lupine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the lupine family and is native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of velvety lupine include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to help with conditions such as arthritis and eczema. Herbal preparations of velvety lupine can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
